title: Man accused of assaulting two workers, stealing car during 3 separate trips to Dedham gas station
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A West Roxbury man allegedly assaulted two employees and stole a car during repeated visits to a Dedham gas station.
Authorities arrested 35-year-old John Laurent, charging him with assault, battery, trespassing, and larceny.
Laurent's arraignment is scheduled for Monday in Dedham District Court after incidents on Saturday at Sam’s Express.

title: Mass. business confidence dips back into pessimism
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts business confidence index fell to 47 in March, down from 52 in February, indicating pessimism.
Surging energy prices and the Iran conflict are major factors contributing to the decline in employer outlooks.
Despite concerns, the U.S. economy showed resilience with 178,000 new jobs added and a stable 4.3% unemployment rate.

title: For $6.5m, this Connecticut inn comes with a liquor license in trendy Mystic
author: Regina Cole
bullets: The Captain's Mansion, a luxury inn in Mystic, CT, is listed for sale at $6.495 million.
Built in 1853, the 10,335 sq ft inn features historic architecture and includes a liquor license.
The property offers eight bedrooms, a commercial kitchen, and is zoned for mixed-use residential and business purposes.

title: Fishing crew rescued near Gloucester after boat runs aground amid harsh seas
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Two crew members were rescued from the fishing boat Legacy after it ran aground near Gloucester amid rough seas.
The Gloucester Fire, Police Departments, and Harbormaster coordinated with the US Coast Guard for the rescue operation.
Rescuers provided survival suits and lines to bring the uninjured crew members safely ashore from the stranded boat.

title: The Rafael Devers trade is already haunting the Red Sox in 2026
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox struggle with a 2-7 start, missing Rafael Devers' impact as lineup lacks power.
Boston's pitching and defense improvements falter; new signing Ranger Suarez starts 0-1 with an 8.64 ERA.
Prospects from Devers trade mostly traded away; James Tibbs excels in Dodgers' system, Kyle Harrison thrives with Brewers.

title: Why it might be time for a slumping Bruins team to call up James Hagens
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are considering calling up James Hagens amid a three-game losing streak and scoring slump.
Hagens, 19, is on an AHL amateur tryout with Providence, recording four points in six games.
Adding Hagens to the NHL roster could potentially boost Boston's struggling power play and overall offensive performance.

title: Tatum, Celtics pull away late to beat Raptors, move closer to 2-seed: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Celtics defeat Raptors 113-95 with strong performances from Jayson Tatum and Jaylen Brown on Easter Sunday.
Center Neemias Queta makes a case for Most Improved Player after contributing 18 points, seven rebounds, and three blocks.
Jordan Walsh provides key contributions off the bench, solidifying his role before Celtics face Hornets and Knicks.

title: New Bedford man gets decades-long prison sentence for raping young relative
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Daniel Turcios Torres sentenced to 36-45 years for raping young relative in New Bedford.
Victim reported assaults to New Bedford police in July 2024 at age 17.
Turcios Torres faces possible ICE detainer after completing prison sentence.

title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, from Boxford, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her late mother who died of brain cancer.
Oakes attributes her mother's extended life to the care received at the MGB cancer center, delaying her passing by four years.
A nurse in oncology, Oakes participates for the second year in the event, representing Massachusetts General Hospital's team.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police shot a man with a sword during a mental health crisis near Northeastern University, he later died.
The man reportedly stabbed an officer and knocked an EMS clinician down; their injuries are non-life-threatening.
Boston police and Suffolk County DA's Office are investigating the shooting, following recent incidents in the area.
